title: Custom-mileage triangulation factors — WHERE × HOW × WHO × NEW/USED × AGE
note: Factors are multiplicative and mean-normalized (VIO-weighted) at runtime so they REDISTRIBUTE miles across models/cohorts without changing the fleet total. base_by_type sets the level (HOW); the WHO/WHERE factors redistribute around it. Collinear signals (income/new-used/geography) are intentionally dampened — see workings/mileage_model_methodology.md.

factor_sources.HOW_type: NHTS/iSeeCars: car ~12,400; SUV ~11,600; pickup ~13,900; motorcycle ~2,259 mi/yr
factor_sources.WHERE_geo: State VMT/driver: Wyoming 21,588 vs DC 6,695. Pickups skew rural/mountain (WY 37% share); EVs/luxury skew urban-coastal/affluent
factor_sources.WHO_income: NHTS: HH >$70k drive ~2x HH <$10k (dampened per-vehicle: high-income HH own more vehicles)
factor_sources.NEWUSED: New-buyer HH income ~$80k vs used ~$48k; new buyers drive more (newer + higher income)

cohort_curves.note: Applied to model-year cohorts (F-150 drill). age_curve = vehicle-age mileage decay (NHTS); newused = residual buyer-channel tilt (new cohorts bought new/higher-income). Combined and VIO-weighted-normalized within a model.

collinearity_dampening.note: income, new-used, geography partly measure the same SES signal. We apply income at full weight, treat new/used as a small residual (cohort level only), and cap the geo factor's deviation. driverage is the most orthogonal of the WHO factors.
